Rollback checklist summary

Short rollback card.

Escalation rollback checklist

Escalation rollback checklists matter when operators need a deterministic sequence for removing temporary scheduling priority after the incident window closes.

The checklist should define verification gates, queue health checks, and dashboard evidence that prove the elevated path can be safely withdrawn.

It should also explain how rollback interacts with fairness targets, fallback queues, and post-incident review controls once priority escalation ends.
